Title: MemorySmith.Agent — IMemoryGateway and MemorySmith REST API
Content:
IMemoryGateway (Agent.Core) has 4 methods: SearchAsync, GetPageAsync, CreatePageAsync, UpdatePageAsync.

RestMemoryGateway (Agent.Memory) implements this via MemorySmith REST endpoints:
  GET /api/search?query={q}&limit=20   -> unified search (pages + memories)
  GET /api/pages/{slug}               -> page content (body field)
  POST /api/pages                     -> create page {slug, title, body, minimumRole}
  PUT /api/pages/{slug}               -> update page

SearchResult.Kind field distinguishes 'page' (slug usable in GetPageAsync) from 'memory' (UUID, NOT a valid page slug).

Config: Agent:Memory:BaseUrl, ApiKey, TimeoutSeconds, DefaultPageRole in appsettings.json.
Auth: X-Api-Key header if ApiKey is configured in MemorySmith.

Phase 2 bug fixed: UpdatePageAsync preserves existing title via GET-then-PUT; falls back gracefully if page doesn't exist.
IHttpClientFactory used for connection pooling (not new HttpClient()).

For Phase 2 tests, use MockMemoryGateway (in MemorySmith.Agent.Tests). No real MemorySmith instance needed for unit tests.
